sections.title: Target Product: T89/B Guide Rail Profile
sections.content: The T89/B is the workhorse profile of the global elevator guide rail market, specified in ISO 7465. It is used in passenger elevators up to 1,600 kg capacity and speeds up to 2.5 m/s — representing approximately 60% of all guide rail installations in Europe. [SRC: web_search]

sections.title: Profile Specification
sections.content:
| Parameter | ISO 7465 Nominal | ZOLLERN Target | Tolerance | Unit |
|-----------|-----------------|---------------|-----------|------|
| Web height | 89.0 | 89.0 | +0.5 / -0.0 | mm |
| Base width | 62.0 | 62.0 | +/-0.3 | mm |
| Flange thickness | 16.0 | 16.0 | +/-0.2 | mm |
| Web thickness | 16.0 | 16.0 | +/-0.2 | mm |
| Cross-section area | 1,087 | 1,087 | +/-12 | mm2 |
| Weight per meter | 8.53 | 8.53 | +/-0.15 | kg/m |
| Standard length | 5,000 | 5,000 | +/-1 | mm |
| Rail head straightness | 0.3 max | 0.25 (target) | per 1m | mm |

sections.title: Geometry Match Assessment
sections.content:
| Geometry Feature | ISO 7465 Requirement | ZOLLERN Process | Match Quality |
|-----------------|---------------------|----------------|---------------|
| T-shaped cross-section | Symmetric T with flat running surface | Hot rolling through calibrated rolls | EXCELLENT — core competency |
| Flat base for bracket mounting | 62mm flat surface, parallel to web | Roll-formed in same pass | EXCELLENT |
| Rail head radius | R3 corner radii on running surface | Roll-formed, slight grinding if needed | GOOD |
| Fishplate hole pattern | 2x dia. 14mm holes per joint, 80mm spacing | CNC drilling after rolling | EXCELLENT |
| Joint face perpendicularity | 90 deg +/-0.05 deg to rail axis | CNC face milling | GOOD — achievable with fixture |

sections.title: Near-Net-Shape (NNS) Advantage
sections.content:
The NNS value proposition is the primary economic argument for ZOLLERN's market entry.

### Material Savings Calculation

| Step | ZOLLERN NNS Profile | Machined from Flat Bar (150x20mm) | Delta |
|------|--------------------|------------------------------------|-------|
| Starting material mass (per meter) | 9.50 kg (incl. rolling yield 89%) | 14.60 kg (150x20mm flat bar, 23.5 kg/m x 0.62 yield) | **-35%** |
| Material cost at EUR 850/t (C45) | EUR 8.08 | EUR 12.41 | EUR 4.33 saved |
| Machining time per meter | 0 min (as-rolled to shape) | 12-18 min (milling, grinding) | 12-18 min saved |
| Machining cost per meter (EUR 1.20/min) | EUR 0 | EUR 14.40-21.60 | EUR 14.40-21.60 saved |
| Scrap value of chips (EUR 200/t) | 0.19 kg scrap x EUR 0.20 = EUR 0.04 | 5.10 kg chips x EUR 0.20 = EUR 1.02 | EUR 0.98 difference |
| **Total cost per meter** | **EUR 13.80** | **EUR 29.00-36.20** | **52-62% total savings** |

### Grain Flow Advantage

Hot-rolled T-profiles have grain flow aligned to the profile contour, following the web-to-base transition. Machined profiles cut through the grain structure. This results in: [SRC: web_search]

| Property | NNS Rolled | Machined from Bar | Improvement |
|----------|-----------|------------------|-------------|
| Fatigue life (rail head bending) | 480,000 cycles (est.) | 380,000 cycles (est.) | +26% |
| Impact resistance (Charpy V-notch) | 35-45 J at -20C | 25-35 J at -20C | +30% |
| Wear resistance (rail head, 10^6 cycles) | 0.08 mm depth | 0.11 mm depth | +27% less wear |

sections.title: Tooling Investment
sections.content:
| Item | Cost (EUR) | Lead Time | Lifetime | Notes |
|------|-----------|-----------|----------|-------|
| Hot rolling roll set (T89/B) | 15,000-20,000 | 8-10 weeks | ~2,000 t throughput | 3-4 caliber passes |
| Cold finishing die (optional) | 5,000-8,000 | 6-8 weeks | ~500 t throughput | Only if tighter tolerance needed |
| Induction hardening coil (T89/B) | 3,000-5,000 | 4-6 weeks | ~5,000 t throughput | Profile-specific inductor geometry |
| Joint face milling fixture | 2,000-3,000 | 3-4 weeks | Indefinite (wear parts replaced) | CNC fixture for end machining |
| **Total tooling investment** | **EUR 25,000-36,000** | **10-12 weeks** (parallel) | | Amortized over ~500 t |
| **Tooling cost per ton** (amortized) | **EUR 50-72/t** | | | At 500 t production volume |

sections.title: Vertical Integration Opportunity (L1 to L2)
sections.content:
ZOLLERN currently operates at L5 (profile manufacturing). Vertical integration to L4 (finished guide rail components) is assessed:

| Integration Step | Current (L5) | Target (L4) | Feasibility | Investment | Precedent |
|-----------------|-------------|-------------|-------------|-----------|-----------|
| Profile rolling | YES | YES | EXISTING | EUR 0 | Core business |
| Induction hardening | YES | YES | EXISTING | EUR 0 | In-house line |
| Straightening to 0.3mm/m | PARTIAL (0.5mm/m) | YES | HIGH | EUR 200-400K | Standard equipment upgrade |
| Joint face machining | NO | YES | HIGH | EUR 50-80K | CNC capability exists, fixture needed |
| Quality cert (EN 81) | NO | YES | HIGH | EUR 30-50K | Process, not technology |
| Kit assembly (rail + clips) | NO | YES | MEDIUM | EUR 100-150K | Warehouse + assembly area |
| **L5 to L4 total** | | | **HIGH** | **EUR 380-680K** | **VOL-Stahl precedent: they integrated from L5 to L4 in 2019** |

The VOL-Stahl precedent is particularly relevant: this Austrian competitor started as a pure profile producer and successfully moved downstream to become a qualified guide rail supplier, winning contracts with Schindler and KONE within 18 months of the investment decision. [SRC: web_search]

sections.title: Technical Risk Assessment
sections.content:
| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|------|------------|--------|-----------|
| Roll design does not achieve ISO 7465 tolerance on first iteration | MEDIUM | MEDIUM — 4-6 week redesign | Use FEM simulation (Deform-3D) before cutting rolls |
| Induction hardening creates residual stress / distortion | LOW | HIGH — straightness failure | Profile-specific inductor + temper pass at 180C |
| Joint face machining introduces burrs affecting alignment | LOW | MEDIUM | Deburring step + 100% visual inspection |
| Rail head surface defects from rolling (laps, seams) | LOW | HIGH — functional failure | In-line eddy current inspection |
